## Verdict: REVISE

## Summary

Round-1 verification: 12 of 13 issues are RESOLVED in the revised plan and one (CRIT-1) is RESOLVED-WITH-A-MINOR-RESIDUAL (no other spawner-aware sections in critic/SKILL.md needed updating, confirmed by direct file read of L11-L29 + L51 + L122). The cost-guard verbatim string with em-dash is consistent across architecture.md L336, current-plan `T-04`(b)/`T-09` acceptance, and the Procedures pseudocode. The recursive-self-critique broadened 4-form alternation correctly fires on this very task. The MAJ-4 alternates (`## Verdict: PASS` and `## Verdict: REVISE`) ARE present in `~/.claude/memory/format-kit.sections.json` allowed_sections (verified by direct python load), so `T-06`'s "no edit expected" claim holds. CRIT-3 / `D-09` correctly removes the dead loop-detection branch from normal-mode pseudocode and gates loop detection on `if strict_mode and round >= 2` (verified at Procedures L274). However, round-2 surfaces ONE NEW CRITICAL issue introduced by the revision (`T-01` anchor places Phase 4 BEFORE `### Output format` — Phase 4 critiques an architecture.md that has not been written yet) and ONE NEW MAJOR (`T-04` acceptance grep ">=3 hits" is structurally a ">=3", not "= 3" — under-specifies how the dead-branch removal is enforced; with the strict-mode loop-detection paragraph still grep-matching, the count is closer to 4 — the acceptance does not detect the regression it was rewritten to detect). Plus three MINOR refinements.

## Issues

### Critical (blocks implementation)

- **CRIT-1 — Phase 4 insertion anchor places critic loop BEFORE the architecture.md write site (Output format), creating an order-of-operations contradiction**
  - What: `T-01`'s anchor explicitly places `### Phase 4: Critic loop` "between line 201 (end of Phase 3 stage decomposition) and line 203 (`### Output format`)". Verified by direct file read: architect/SKILL.md L184-L201 is Phase 3 prose; L203 is `### Output format` heading. The Output format section (L203-L269) is where Steps 1-6 of the §5.3 Class B writer mechanism actually WRITE architecture.md to disk (Step 6 atomic-rename at L269). With the proposed insertion order, Phase 4 would execute BEFORE architecture.md exists on disk — but the Procedures pseudocode at L255-294 says "spawn_critic_subagent(model=opus, target=abs_path_to_architecture_md)" and "read architecture-critic-{round}.md" — the critic would read a non-existent file.
  - Why it matters: The architecture document's own pseudocode (architecture.md L172-L202) explicitly says "After writing architecture.md, spawn /critic --target=architecture.md" — i.e., the intended order is (Phase 1 scan) → (Phase 2 synthesize) → (Phase 3 decompose) → (Output format writes architecture.md) → (Phase 4 critic loop, which on REVISE re-invokes Output format Steps 1-6 with critic feedback). The current `T-01` anchor inverts this. Any /implement that follows `T-01` verbatim will produce a broken phase ordering: critic targets a path that has not been created. Even if the implementer "fixes" this silently, the plan is misleading and the acceptance grep (`grep -n '^### Phase 4:'` returns 1, AFTER `### Phase 3:` and BEFORE `### Output format`) actively REWARDS the wrong placement. This is exactly the lesson 2026-04-23 "rubric refs at write-site" pattern in reverse: spawn-site for the critic must be AFTER the artifact-it-critiques is written.
  - Where: `T-01` Anchor bullet (current-plan L43); `T-01` acceptance bullet 2 (current-plan L47); Procedures pseudocode (current-plan L255-294); architecture.md L172-L202 pseudocode (the source of truth that contradicts `T-01`).
  - Suggestion: Re-anchor `T-01` to insert Phase 4 IMMEDIATELY AFTER the `### Output format` section (i.e., after L269 `### Output format` Step 6 atomic rename, BEFORE L271 `## Save session state`). Update `T-01` acceptance to: "`### Phase 4:` heading appears AFTER `### Output format` Step 6 (atomic rename) and BEFORE `## Save session state` (verify line numbers monotonic)." Also add to `T-01` body content guidance: "Phase 4 entry assumes architecture.md exists on disk at the path returned by Output format Step 6; the loop's REVISE branch invokes Output format Steps 1-6 again with the prior architecture.md as input plus the critic feedback to re-synthesize." This change is anchor-only — the Procedures pseudocode at L255-294 is correct as written; only `T-01`'s insertion-point claim and acceptance criterion need to be re-anchored. Optionally update `D-03` to make the call-back-into-Output-format Steps 1-6 explicit (currently `D-03` says "re-runs synthesis" — the precise mechanic is "calls Output format Steps 1-6 with the prior body + critic feedback").

### Major (significant gap, should address)

- **MAJ-1 — `T-04` acceptance grep cardinality ">=3 hits" under-specifies the CRIT-3/`D-09` dead-branch removal; the regression the rewrite was meant to enforce is not enforceable**
  - What: `T-04` acceptance bullet 1 reads: "`grep -nE 'max_rounds|cost guard|recursive.self.critique' dev-workflow/skills/architect/SKILL.md` returns ≥3 hits in normal-mode Phase 4 pseudocode; loop detection appears ONLY in the strict-mode section." Per `D-09` / CRIT-3, the dead-branch removal of `if round >= 2` from the normal-mode pseudocode is the substantive change. But the grep alternation is `max_rounds|cost guard|recursive.self.critique` — i.e., it hits any of three independent tokens, possibly each multiple times (the word `max_rounds` appears in many places — pseudocode comments, parsing logic, the convergence-rules section). With ">=3" as the floor and three patterns each potentially matching multiple times in a real SKILL.md prose body, the floor is essentially "anything > 2", and the test cannot DETECT regression: if a future implementer accidentally re-introduces the dead `if round >= 2` branch in normal-mode pseudocode, this grep would still pass.
  - Why it matters: The acceptance is supposed to enforce "loop detection appears ONLY in the strict-mode section" — but the grep pattern does not even match the string "loop detection" (the existing pattern matches max_rounds, cost guard, recursive.self.critique). The "appears ONLY in strict-mode section" clause is asserted in prose but has no matching grep. A reviewer/implementer reading the acceptance might think the grep enforces this, but it does not. This is the same class as the stage-3 round-4 audit-grep narrowness pattern: an acceptance grep that does not actually test what its prose claims to test.
  - Where: `T-04` Acceptance bullet 1 (current-plan L86-87).
  - Suggestion: Split `T-04` acceptance into two assertions:
    - **Positive**: `awk '/^### Phase 4:/,/^### |^## /' dev-workflow/skills/architect/SKILL.md | grep -cE '\bmax_rounds\b'` returns ≥1 AND `... | grep -cF 'cost guard'` returns ≥1 AND `... | grep -cE 'recursive.self.critique'` returns ≥1 (three SEPARATE assertions, each ≥1 hit, scoped to the Phase 4 section only via awk).
    - **Negative**: `awk '/^### Phase 4:/,/^## Save session state/' dev-workflow/skills/architect/SKILL.md | grep -cE 'if[[:space:]]+round[[:space:]]*>=?[[:space:]]*2'` returns exactly 1 hit (the strict-mode-gated branch — which appears as `if strict_mode and round >= 2` per Procedures L274) — NOT 0 (because strict-mode loop detection legitimately uses this guard) and NOT 2+ (which would indicate the dead normal-mode branch was re-introduced). Combined with a separate `... | grep -cE '^[[:space:]]*if[[:space:]]+round[[:space:]]*>=' ` assertion of exactly 0 hits for un-gated `if round >=` patterns, this provides a concrete regression detector for the `D-09` dead-branch removal. Without this, the prose claim of "appears ONLY in strict-mode section" is unverified.

### Minor (improvement, use judgment)

- **MIN-1 — CRIT-1 (round-1) fix wording leaves standalone-invocation case unaddressed (not a regression — preserves prior ambiguity)**
  - Suggestion: `T-02` sub-task 2b updates critic/SKILL.md L25-27 to "When invoked as part of `/thorough_plan` OR by `/architect` Phase 4, you MUST run in a fresh agent session." The skill description (frontmatter L3) says `/critic` "Can be used standalone or as part of /thorough_plan orchestration." After the fix, the wording still does not address what fresh-context expectations apply for standalone user invocations. Pre-existing wording had the same ambiguity (it just enumerated /thorough_plan), so this is not a CRIT-1 regression. Consider broadening to: "When invoked as part of `/thorough_plan`, by `/architect` Phase 4, or as a standalone user invocation against an existing plan, you MUST run in a fresh agent session." This is suggestion-level only — current scope is acceptable.

- **MIN-2 — `T-08` Phase 4 cache-write-through note is positionally orphaned by the CRIT-1 anchor fix**
  - Suggestion: `T-08` says "add a one-line note in the new Phase 4 body" with a specific cache-write-through grep target. After the CRIT-1 anchor re-placement (Phase 4 moves to AFTER Output format), the `T-08` note still lands in the new Phase 4 section — no edit needed. But the rationale ("Phase 4 produces architecture-critic-N.md ... no cache write-through required") becomes more obviously correct because Phase 4 is now post-Output-format. Add a one-clause cross-reference in `T-08` prose: "Phase 4 invokes Output format Steps 1-6 on REVISE re-synthesis; the cache-write-through obligation for that re-synthesis follows the existing Output format Step 1-6 contract (which has none, since architecture.md is not source)." This makes the closure explicit instead of implicit.

- **MIN-3 — `R-N` "merged into `R-G`" row retained as audit-trail breaks v3 format-kit table-row integrity**
  - Suggestion: The Risks table includes `R-N` as a present row with "Merged into `R-G` per MIN-5 fix" content. Per format-kit conventions (and the V-04 fenced-code/table integrity), a "merged-into-X" row in a live risks table is a hybrid contract+audit-trail use. Two cleaner options: (a) move the merge-note to the Notes section ("`R-N` (formerly: ...) merged into `R-G` — see `R-G` mitigation column for the specific allowed_sections regression guard") and remove `R-N` from the table entirely; (b) keep `R-N` as a table row but mark it "STATUS: merged" in a new column to make the table entry semantically distinct from a live risk. Current state — a row that says "Merged into `R-G`" while still appearing as a table row — risks confusion at /implement time. Cosmetic, not blocking.

## What's good

- **CRIT-1 (round-1) verified resolved precisely**: critic/SKILL.md L25-L27 wording was verified by direct file read; `T-02` sub-task 2b acceptance grep `thorough_plan.*OR.*architect|architect.*Phase 4` correctly targets the L25-L27 region; lesson 2026-04-13 (target SKILL.md must be updated) is closed inline. No other spawner-aware sections in critic/SKILL.md needed updating (verified by reading L11-29 + the Process section L29-L120; round detection is already addressed by `T-03`).
- **CRIT-2 / `D-06` broadened 4-form alternation correctly fires on this very task**: I verified by reading current-plan.md and architecture.md — both reference `dev-workflow/skills/(architect|critic)/SKILL.md` and `~/.claude/skills/(architect|critic)/SKILL.md` forms. The recursive-self-critique guard would correctly fire on stage-4's own architecture.md, validating `Q-01` closure.
- **CRIT-3 / `D-09` dead-branch removal is correctly explicit**: Procedures pseudocode (L274) shows `if strict_mode and round >= 2:` — the gated branch is clearly strict-mode-only. The plan also added explicit prose "loop detection is a strict-mode-only feature; normal mode relies on max_rounds=2 hard cap" — this prose closure is good (separate concern with the acceptance grep — see MAJ-1 above).
- **MAJ-4 verified: format-kit.sections.json `## Verdict: PASS` and `## Verdict: REVISE` ARE in critic-response allowed_sections** (verified by python load of `~/.claude/memory/format-kit.sections.json` — output contains `'## Verdict: PASS'` and `'## Verdict: REVISE'`). `T-06`'s "no edit expected" claim holds; the MAJ-4 Python one-liner is a correct regression guard for a future schema change.
- **MAJ-5 acceptance rewrite is concrete and testable**: `T-09` now specifies (a) hand-filled markdown grep counts, (b) string-match all three form variants, (c) `grep -F` for em-dash exact match. The "exits 0" mistake is gone. Mirrors stage-3 `T-09` grep-battery pattern correctly.
- **MAJ-1 (round-1) — `T-12` (/run integration) is well-scoped**: 4 explicit edits to run/SKILL.md Phase 2 + Checkpoint A; acceptance grep `Phase 4|architecture-critic` ≥2 is appropriate. /run Phase 3 (thorough_plan) is correctly out-of-scope (Phase 4 critic in /architect does not affect /thorough_plan downstream).
- **MAJ-2 model:opus assertion is now testable**: `T-01` acceptance includes `grep -nE 'model[\s:=]+"?opus'` with awk line-range scoping. Pseudocode at Procedures L263 uses `spawn_critic_subagent(model="opus", ...)` form (consistent with /thorough_plan SKILL.md L184 prose convention).
- **MAJ-3 single-stage / multi-stage parity is explicit in `D-03` corollary AND `T-10` covers BOTH variants**: architecture-critic-N.md ALWAYS at task root regardless of stage layout. Architecture-side already encoded at architect/SKILL.md L304 HTML comment.
- **All 3 open questions are CLOSED** (`Q-01` RESOLVED via `D-06`; `Q-02` RESOLVED via `D-07`; `Q-03` confirmed via `T-10` fixture name `phase-4-smoke`).
- **Cost-guard verbatim em-dash string consistency**: same exact ASCII em-dash string `[critic round 2 starting — ~$10-30 estimated based on body size]` in architecture.md L336, current-plan `T-04`(b), `T-04` acceptance, `T-09` acceptance, `R-E` mitigation, and Procedures L259. No drift.
- **Cost ledger has the round-2 revise row** at line 43 (`83e72041-... | revise | claude-sonnet-4-6`), confirming Sonnet `/revise-fast` was used per Medium-profile defaults.
